title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new co-working space in their city.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve started using that 'Hub-360' space near the station. It’s got a much better vibe than my home office. || Woman: [1]I was impressed by the ergonomics of the workstations[/1]. It’s the first time I haven’t had a backache after a long day of coding. || Man: The chairs are top-tier. But [2]I found the booking system for the meeting rooms to be a bit of a nightmare[/2]. It kept crashing right when I needed to confirm a client call. || Woman: I had the same issue! It’s surprisingly glitchy for a tech-focused space. || Man: Exactly. For the price of the monthly pass, you’d expect the software to actually work. || Woman: Hopefully they’ll patch it soon, otherwise people will just head back to the local café.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve started using that 'Hub-360' space near the station. It’s got a much better vibe than my home office.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I was impressed by the ergonomics of the workstations. It’s the first time I haven’t had a backache after a long day of coding.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The chairs are top-tier. But I found the booking system for the meeting rooms to be a bit of a nightmare. It kept crashing right when I needed to confirm a client call.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I had the same issue! It’s surprisingly glitchy for a tech-focused space.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Exactly. For the price of the monthly pass, you’d expect the software to actually work.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Hopefully they’ll patch it soon, otherwise people will just head back to the local café.

questions.question: What does the woman like most about the co-working space?
questions.explanation: She praises the 'ergonomics of the workstations' and mentions her back doesn't ache [1].

questions.question: Both speakers agree that the facility's digital interface is
questions.explanation: They both complain about the booking system 'crashing' and being 'glitchy' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new mental health initiative at work.
extracts.audio_script: Man: It’s good to see the company finally offering free counselling sessions. It shows they care about more than just the bottom line. || Woman: [3]I suspect it’s a bit of a defensive move to reduce the number of staff taking long-term sick leave[/3], to be honest. || Man: Maybe, but the effect is the same. People get the support they need. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that the 'quiet room' they’ve designated is right next to the noisy lift shaft[/4], which seems to defeat the purpose. || Man: I agree. It’s hard to meditate when you can hear the machinery clunking every two minutes. || Woman: It’s a well-intentioned idea, but it feels like it was planned by someone who’s never actually tried to relax in this office.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It’s good to see the company finally offering free counselling sessions. It shows they care about more than just the bottom line.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect it’s a bit of a defensive move to reduce the number of staff taking long-term sick leave, to be honest.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Maybe, but the effect is the same. People get the support they need.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that the 'quiet room' they’ve designated is right next to the noisy lift shaft, which seems to defeat the purpose.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s hard to meditate when you can hear the machinery clunking every two minutes.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s a well-intentioned idea, but it feels like it was planned by someone who’s never actually tried to relax in this office.

questions.question: The woman believes the initiative was primarily driven by

options.A: a genuine concern for employee welfare.
options.B: a desire to improve the firm's efficiency.

questions.explanation: She thinks it's a move to 'reduce the number of staff taking long-term sick leave' [3].

questions.question: What practical issue do both speakers identify?

options.A: the lack of available counselling slots
options.B: the inappropriate location of a new facility
options.C: the poor quality of the meditation sessions

questions.explanation: They both agree the 'quiet room' is too noisy because it's next to the 'lift shaft' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new documentary about the history of the internet.
extracts.audio_script: Man: That film about the early days of the web was fascinating. I’d forgotten how clunky those first websites were! || Woman: [5]I appreciated how they highlighted the contributions of the lesser-known pioneers[/5], rather than just focusing on the big names like Bill Gates. || Man: I agree. It gave a much fairer picture of how collaborative the whole process was. || Woman: [6]I did think the soundtrack was a bit overbearing, though[/6]. That dramatic music during the 'browser wars' section was a bit much. || Man: It was a bit 'theatrical', I suppose. But overall, it was a very solid piece of filmmaking. || Woman: Definitely. It’s rare to find a tech documentary that’s actually accessible to people who aren't engineers.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That film about the early days of the web was fascinating. I’d forgotten how clunky those first websites were!

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I appreciated how they highlighted the contributions of the lesser-known pioneers, rather than just focusing on the big names like Bill Gates.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It gave a much fairer picture of how collaborative the whole process was.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did think the soundtrack was a bit overbearing, though. That dramatic music during the 'browser wars' section was a bit much.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It was a bit 'theatrical', I suppose. But overall, it was a very solid piece of filmmaking.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. It’s rare to find a tech documentary that’s actually accessible to people who aren't engineers.

questions.question: What did the woman value about the documentary?

options.A: the high quality of the archival footage
options.C: the clear explanation of technical terms

questions.explanation: She liked that they 'highlighted the contributions of the lesser-known pioneers' [5].

questions.question: The woman's reaction to the music in the film was that

options.B: it helped to maintain the pacing.

questions.explanation: She calls the soundtrack 'overbearing' and the music 'a bit much' [6].
